query: Playground: Earn $100K+ in weekly rewards for hacking AI agents. alternative

results.title: AI Agent & Infra Hackathon - Devpost
results.content:
Machine Learning/AI   Serverless

#### About the challenge

​Join us at the AI Agent & Infra Hackathon, co-hosted by Lux Capital, Modal, Cognition, and AWS. Spend 36 hours building at the cutting edge of AI/agents/infra, turn ambitious ideas into working demos, compete for $100K+ in prizes, and connect directly with top VCs and builders.

​Apply by August 10 11:59 PM (EST), applications accepted on a rolling basis.

All final decisions will be sent by August 11 EOD.

#### More Details

Who Can Attend?

​This hackathon is open to college students (undergrad, grad, PhDs), early-career professionals, and engineers/researchers with an interest in AI, infrastructure, and agents. Teams can include 1–2 people. Each member must apply individually, but teams will be accepted as a unit. [...] ​Best Agent Hack, sponsored by Cognition

- 1st Place: $3,000 + 1 year of Windsurf Pro + 1 year of Devin Team (total valued ~$9,000)  
- 2nd Place: $1,500 + 1 year of Windsurf Pro + 1 year of Devin Team (total valued ~$7,500)  
- 3rd Place: $500 + 1 year of Windsurf Pro + 1 year of Devin Team (total valued ~$6,500)

​Description: Any project building an agent or a custom MCP client or server.

​Note: All participants are automatically considered for Best Overall Hack. Upon registration, hackers must choose whether to compete in the Best Use of Modal, Best Agent Hack, or both. This selection is binding, and hackers must satisfy the description of the track they register for.

​Credits

​Participants accepted to the Best Use of Modal track will receive $500 in Modal credits. [...] ​Participants accepted to the Best Agent Hack track will get access to the Devin Core Plan and 3 months of Windsurf Pro (valued at $15/month).

### Hackathon Sponsors

### Prizes

3 non-cash prizes

###### Best Overall hack

3 winners

Sponsored by AWS and Lux Capital

- 1st Place: $25,000 AWS credits & Lux Capital Partner Meeting

- 2nd Place: $10,000 AWS credits & Lux Capital Partner Meeting

- 3rd Place: $5,000 AWS credits & Lux Capital Partner Meeting

###### Best Use of Modal

3 winners

Sponsored by Modal Labs   
- 1st Place: $25,000 Modal credits   
- 2nd Place: $10,000 Modal credits   
- 3rd Place: $1,000 Modal credits

###### Best Agent Hack

3 winners

- 1st Place: $3,000 + 1 year of Windsurf Pro + 1 year of Devin Team (total valued ~$9,000)

results.title: Fabraix: The world's frontier hacker for AI agents.
results.content:
Our first challenge, The Gatekeeper, is live now. Kai is an assistant guarding a classified access code. You can start playing without an account, but you’ll need to sign in for a successful break to count toward the weekly leaderboard. We review every submission ourselves. The player with the most approved breaks each week wins, and we publish a new challenge every Monday.

Playground is open source, including the client, a reference implementation of the defender engine, and every challenge configuration. You can also propose a future challenge.

We made Playground public because other people will try attacks our team would never think of. We’ll use what we learn from successful breaks to improve how AI agents are tested and defended. [...] What do you think? …

Zach
Fabraix

Fabraix

Hey Product Hunt 👋 Zach here, co-founder of Fabraix.

We build frontier red-teaming AI agents that find security vulnerabilities in customer-facing AI. Playground turns part of that work into a game anyone can play.

Each challenge is a live AI agent with real tools, including web search and browser access. It has a secret it has been instructed to protect, and we publish its full system prompt. You can see exactly what the agent was told and try to get around its defenses. [...] Show us your hacking skills → playground.fabraix.com

Sabber Ahamed

"approved breaks" is the interesting phrase, who approves them, a human reviewer or a judge model? grading jailbreak success automatically is its own hard problem, curious if that's solved or still manual.

Zach
Fabraix

Fabraix

Laiba

This is a fascinating idea. We spend so much time optimizing AI agents, but not nearly enough time trying to break them.

Curious...what's the most surprising vulnerability you've uncovered so far?

Wishing you a successful launch! 🚀

Ibrahim Abdu
Fabraix

Fabraix

@worksforme Thanks!! Yeah thats our thesis!

Ibrahim Abdu
Fabraix

Fabraix

@worksforme We've got a crazy one launching this week - browser agent leaking a users email and password by replying to an email!

Laiba

The complete guide to white-labeling AI agents for clients. Step-by-step playbook covering platform selection, niche targeting, white-label setup, monetization, and scaling to $100K+ per year.

I compared 12 no-code AI agent builders specifically for consultants and agencies -- evaluating white-labeling, monetization, client deployment, and pricing to find what actually works for building a services business.

Pickaxe is a no-code platform for building, deploying, and monetizing custom AI tools. [...] The step-by-step playbook for starting an AI agent agency in 2026 — from picking your niche and pricing your services to landing clients and scaling beyond solo.

Illustration of a small adventurer hanging their own banner on glowing market stalls in a sunny meadow, Ghibli style — white-label AI tools for agencies

## 9 White-Label AI Tools for Agencies: What We'd Actually Use

An honest comparison of 9 white-label AI tools agencies can rebrand and resell in 2026 — from full SaaS-mode platforms to budget chatbot resellers, with real pricing and the catches nobody mentions.

White-labeling AI for clients - building an AI agency with Pickaxe

## White-Labeling AI for Clients: The Exact Playbook I'd Use to Build a $100K/Month AI Agency [...] Pickaxe
Pickaxe

# How to Monetize AI Agents in 2026: The Complete Playbook

## Chris Peterson

1 month ago

### On this page

Illustration of a small adventurer opening a glowing treasure chest filled with golden coins on a hillside overlooking a vast green valley, representing AI agent monetization opportunities

We're past the "build cool AI demos" phase. In 2026, the question isn't whether AI agents work — it's who's making money from them and how.

I've had a front-row seat to this shift. Running Pickaxe, I've watched thousands of creators, consultants, and small agencies go from tinkering with chatbots to generating real, recurring revenue from AI agents. Some hit $5K/month within 90 days. Others stall out and wonder why.

results.title: Best AI agent monetization platforms | MintMCP Blog
results.content:
### Investment requirements​

 $50K to $100K+ annually for enterprise implementations
 4 to 8 weeks implementation with professional services
 Significant internal resources required for configuration

Best For: Large enterprises with complex revenue recognition requirements, substantial budgets, and existing finance infrastructure requiring enterprise-grade compliance features.

## Why nevermined stands out for AI agent monetization​

### Competitive edge: Agent-Native vs. Retrofitted solutions​

The fundamental difference between Nevermined and traditional billing platforms lies in architecture. Nevermined was built from the ground up for autonomous AI agent commerce, while traditional platforms retrofit existing subscription and invoice infrastructure to handle AI workloads. [...] ### Flex credits for predictable spend​

Flex Credits operate as prepaid consumption-based units redeemed directly against usage. Credits solve multiple problems:

 Align price to value by charging for micro-actions and rewarding successful outcomes
 Enable flexible scaling where credits can be reallocated across users, departments, or agents without renegotiating licenses
 Provide predictable spend where users prepay credits, monitor burn rate in real-time, and avoid surprise overruns

### Quick wins​

Valory cut deployment time of their payments and billing infrastructure for the Olas AI agent marketplace from 6 weeks to 6 hours using Nevermined, clawing back $1000s in engineering costs.
